questions.question: A carpenter has $48$ feet of wood to build a treehouse. If she wants to make the sides of the treehouse a square with area $16$ square feet, what is the length of each side in feet?
A clock strikes $12$ times. If the time between strikes is $3$ seconds and the first strike is at $12:00$, what is the total time elapsed until the last strike in seconds?
A snail is at the bottom of a $20$-foot well. Each day, it climbs $3$ feet, but at night, it slips back $2$ feet. How many days will it take for the snail to reach the top of the well?

A bakery is making a special cake with a layered square design, where each layer has an area of $64$ square inches. If they want to add a border around the cake, increasing its area by $25\%$, what is the area of the larger square in square inches?

A person wants to save money for a new bike and decides to save $5$ dollars per day for $30$ days. If the person then decides to invest this money into a savings account that earns an annual interest rate of $2\%$, how much money will they have after $1$ year, assuming the interest compounds annually?

teacher_rules: Tip 1: Check if students simplify radicals correctly
Tip 2: Make sure students can convert between radical and rational exponent form
Tip 3: Ensure students can apply radical concepts to real-world scenarios
